purpose: Read-only accessor for the queue/ overflow directory used by !master_plan_track when more swarms are planned than the active dashboard slots can hold. Mirrors DashboardService's read API (sync + async pairs for init/logs/progress) but rooted at QUEUE_DIR. listQueue() returns sorted queue ids whose directory has an initialization.json (validity gate). listQueueSummaries() is the showpiece function: for each queue item, computes a derived `status` string ('pending' | 'in_progress' | 'completed' | 'error') by walking progress entries — accounts for partially-written queues by comparing progressValues.length vs init.task.total_tasks and only declares completed when ALL tasks are accounted for AND none are in progress. Returns lightweight summaries for the QueuePopup UI.
gotchas: listQueueSummaries derives status WITHOUT consulting init.agents — it walks the progress map only. If a queue item's progress dir has 5 files and init.task.total_tasks is 3 (mismatch), allDone evaluates to true based on those 5 files. Cross-check init.task.total_tasks is the only guard against premature completion.

The completion test uses BOTH (a) `progressValues.length < totalTasks` to flag incomplete AND (b) per-progress status walk. A queue item where total_tasks is 0 or missing skips guard (a) — so a queue with 1 progress file marked 'completed' and total_tasks:0 would report 'completed' even if more tasks were planned.

Status priority: error trumps completed, completed trumps in_progress, in_progress trumps pending. If a worker is in_progress AND a sibling already failed, status is 'in_progress' (not 'error') until allDone — UI may not reflect the failure until the queue finishes.
fs.promises is lazy-required inside readQueueProgressAsync — avoids loading promises module at import time but minor cost per call. Keep as-is to match symmetry with the sync variant.
No write API — queue items are written by master agents directly via filesystem (per master-protocol). This service is read-only by design.
listQueue sort is plain `.sort()` (lexicographic) — not numeric. queue10 sorts before queue2.
patterns: Mirror of DashboardService API surface — same function names with `Queue` prefix. Maintained intentionally so consumers can swap dashboards/queue accessors with minimal change.
Three-state derivation (pending/in_progress/completed/error) computed live from progress walk rather than stored — queue items have no separate status field, status is always derived.
Validity gate via initialization.json existsSync — same pattern as DashboardService.listDashboards.
conventions: Read-only module, sync + async parallel pairs (matches DashboardService convention).
Lightweight summary shape: { id, task: {name, type, directory, total_tasks, created} | null, agentCount, status } — used directly by QueuePopup.jsx without further transformation.
Defensive try/catch returning [] / {} from each function — never throws.
